title: Committed review inspects overlap surfaces for new resources before findings freeze

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark deterministic overlap-surface coverage for new resources so reruns over the same PR do not drift based on the active editor file.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models the historical drift where the active file is the new group-managed resource, but the critical defect sits on the unchanged legacy single-item surface `internal/services/example/example_item_resource.go`. The deterministic router must still add that sibling surface to the coverage matrix because it can manage the same remote object.

description: A sanitized committed-review scenario where a pull request adds a new provider resource, but the real defect lives on a pre-existing sibling surface that can still manage the same remote object. The correct review must build a deterministic coverage matrix, inspect the unchanged sibling lifecycle windows, and surface the overlap issue regardless of which new file is active in the editor.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models the modeled drift where the active file is the new group-managed resource, but the critical defect sits on the unchanged legacy single-item surface `internal/services/example/example_item_resource.go`. The deterministic router must still add that sibling surface to the coverage matrix because it can manage the same remote object.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should inspect unchanged sibling ownership surfaces when a new resource overlaps existing management paths, rather than limiting review to the new files alone.

The committed review should check import/read/update/delete mode-gating symmetry on the overlapping sibling surface and surface a missing guard when create or update is protected but importer, read, or delete is not.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not let the active new-resource file suppress inspection of unchanged overlapping sibling surfaces.

notes: Use this case to benchmark deterministic overlap-surface coverage for new resources so reruns over the same PR do not drift based on the active editor file.
